lessons.name: Review 1 (Units 1 - 2 - 3)
lessons.description: Review of Units 1, 2, and 3 covering local community, city life, and healthy living.

sections.heading: 1. Language - Vocabulary
sections.content:
Unit 1: Local Community

Key vocabulary: community center, library, craft village, pottery, weaving, embroidery, volunteer, donate, preserve, traditional, cultural, historical

Unit 2: City Life

Key vocabulary: skyscraper, traffic, public transport, metro, pollution, crowded, convenient, modern, opportunity, infrastructure

Unit 3: Healthy Living for Teens

Key vocabulary: healthy, fit, exercise, balanced diet, nutrition, stress, pressure, anxiety, junk food, sleep, relax

sections.heading: 2. Language - Grammar
sections.content:
Unit 1: Question Words + to-infinitive & Phrasal Verbs

Structure: Wh-word + to-infinitive (what to do, where to go, how to use)

Phrasal verbs: look after, look for, set up, find out, carry out

Unit 2: Double Comparatives & Phrasal Verbs

Structure: The + comparative + S + V, the + comparative + S + V

Phrasal verbs: get around, grow up, deal with, run out of, keep up with

Unit 3: Modal Verbs in Conditional Type 1

Structure: If + S + V (present), S + modal verb + V

Modal verbs: can, may, might, should, must

Unless: Unless = If not

sections.heading: 3. Skills - Reading Passages
sections.content:
Passage 1: Community and City Life

I grew up in a small village with a traditional craft community. People made pottery and embroidery. I didn't know what to do after school, so I learned how to make pottery. My teacher showed me where to find clay and how to use the wheel.

Now I live in a big city. The bigger the city is, the more opportunities there are. However, the more crowded it becomes, the more difficult it is to get around. I often look for quiet places to relax. It's hard to keep up with the fast pace of city life.

Passage 2: Healthy Living

If you want to stay healthy, you should exercise regularly and eat a balanced diet. If you don't sleep enough, you may feel tired and stressed. The more you exercise, the stronger you become.

Unless you take care of your health, you can't enjoy life. If you feel stressed, you should find out ways to relax. Many teens don't know how to deal with pressure. They should learn what to do when they feel anxious.

sections.heading: 4. Skills - Practice
sections.content:
Comprehension Questions:

  1. What did the writer learn in the village?
  2. What happens when the city becomes more crowded?
  3. What should you do if you want to stay healthy?
  4. What happens if you don't sleep enough?

examples.question: The bigger the city, the ______ (crowded) it is.
examples.solution: The bigger the city, the more crowded it is.

problemTypes.name: Dạng 3: Modal Verbs in Conditional Type 1
problemTypes.method: Phương pháp: If + S + V, S + modal + V

examples.question: If you exercise, you ______ (can / stay) fit.
examples.solution: If you exercise, you can stay fit.

problemTypes.name: Dạng 4: Phrasal Verbs
problemTypes.method: Phương pháp: Học thuộc nghĩa phrasal verbs

exercises.question: I don't know ______ to do.
She asked me ______ to go.
He showed me ______ to use it.
exercises.explanation: how to use = cách sử dụng

exercises.question: The bigger the city is, the ______ it is.
The ______ you drive, the more dangerous it is.
The more expensive the city is, the ______ life is.
If you exercise regularly, you ______ stay fit.
If you don't sleep enough, you ______ feel tired.
If you feel stressed, you ______ relax.
Unless you exercise, you ______ stay fit.
She ______ her younger brother.
I'm ______ my keys.
I ______ in Hanoi.
It's easy to ______ in this city.
We need to ______ this problem.
They ______ a new business.
I can't decide ______ to choose.
exercises.explanation: which to choose = chọn cái nào

exercises.question: The older I get, the ______ I become.
If you are sick, you ______ see a doctor.
We need to ______ the truth.
Which are correct question words + to-infinitive? (Select all that apply)
exercises.explanation: Đúng: what to do, where to go, how to use. Sai: why to come

exercises.question: Which are correct double comparatives? (Select all that apply)
options: The more expensive, the more difficult.
exercises.explanation: Đúng: bigger/better, more expensive/more difficult, hotter/more tired. Sai: dangerouser

exercises.question: Which modal verbs express advice? (Select all that apply)
exercises.explanation: should và ought to = nên (lời khuyên)

exercises.question: Fill in: I don't know ______ (what / do).
Fill in: The ______ (big) the city, the ______ (crowded) it is.
Fill in: If you exercise, you ______ (can / stay) fit.
Fill in phrasal verb: She ______ (chăm sóc) her brother.
Rearrange: know / I / don't / what / do / to / . / bigger / The / city / the / is / , / more / the / crowded / is / it / .
exercises.sample_answer: I don't know what to do. The bigger the city is, the more crowded it is.
exercises.explanation: Sắp xếp: question word + to V; double comparative

exercises.question: Transform: I don't know. What should I do? → I don't know ______.
exercises.sample_answer: I don't know what to do.
exercises.explanation: Biến đổi: What should I do? → what to do

exercises.question: Transform: The city is big. It is crowded. → The ______, the ______.
exercises.sample_answer: The bigger the city is, the more crowded it is.
exercises.explanation: Biến đổi: big → bigger; crowded → more crowded

exercises.question: Transform: If you don't exercise, you can't stay fit. → Unless ______.
exercises.sample_answer: Unless you exercise, you can't stay fit.

exercises.question: Combine all grammar: I don't know how to get around. The bigger the city, the harder it is. If you exercise, you can stay fit. (Keep all three sentences)
exercises.sample_answer: I don't know how to get around. The bigger the city, the harder it is. If you exercise, you can stay fit.
exercises.explanation: Kết hợp: question word + to V, double comparative, conditional type 1 với modal verb
